parentQuestion: An individual who could trace a picture of a bicycle with their finger but could...

scaffolds.q: Seeing an object and recognizing what it is are two different brain processes.
scaffolds.explain: The brain processes vision in stages: first detecting shapes and features (primary visual cortex), then identifying what those shapes mean (association areas). You can have one without the other.

scaffolds.q: What term describes being unable to recognize objects despite being able to see them?
scaffolds.explain: Agnosia means 'without knowledge' — the inability to recognize or interpret sensory information despite intact sensation. Visual agnosia specifically affects visual recognition. You see perfectly. You understand nothing.
